Adapter.description: Configuration for a custom canister build adapter.

oneOf.description: Command used to build a canister
Set of commands used to build a canister

anyOf.description: Local path on-disk to read a WASM file from
Remote url to fetch a WASM file from

Adapter2.description:
Configuration for a wasm source — used by adapters that load a `.wasm` file
either from a local path or from a remote URL.

sha256.description: Optional sha256 checksum of the WASM

Adapter3.description: Configuration for a custom canister build adapter.

oneOf.description: Directory used to synchronize an assets canister
Set of directories used to synchronize an assets canister

anyOf.description: Local path on-disk to read a WASM file from
Remote url to fetch a WASM file from

Adapter4.description:
Configuration for a sync plugin step.

A sync plugin is a WebAssembly module invoked during `icp sync` for a
specific canister. It runs inside a WASI sandbox whose filesystem access
is limited to the directories listed in `dirs` (preopened read-only) plus
the contents of any files listed in `files` (read by the host and passed
inline to the plugin).

Example (local path):
```yaml
- type: plugin
  path: ./plugins/populate-data.wasm
  sha256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afb...   # optional for path
  dirs:                               # directories preopened read-only
    - assets/seed-data
  files:                              # files read by the host and passed inline
    - config.txt
```

Example (remote URL — `sha256` is required):
```yaml
- type: plugin
  url: https://example.com/plugins/populate-data.wasm
  sha256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afb...   # required for url
```

dirs.description:
Directories (relative to canister directory) the plugin may read from.
Each entry must be a directory; it is preopened via WASI so the plugin
can traverse it using standard filesystem APIs.

files.description:
Files (relative to canister directory) the host reads and passes to
the plugin as part of `sync-exec-input.files`.

sha256.description:
Optional sha256 checksum of the wasm file.
Optional for `path`; required for `url`.

ArgsFormat.description: Format specifier for canister call/install args content.

oneOf.description: Raw binary (only valid for file references)

BuildStep.description:
Identifies the type of adapter used to build the canister,
along with its configuration.

The adapter type is specified via the `type` field in the YAML file.
For example:

```yaml
type: script
command: do_something.sh
```

oneOf.description:
Represents a canister built using a custom script or command.
This variant allows for flexible build processes defined by the user.

Represents a pre-built canister.
This variant allows for retrieving a canister WASM from various sources.

BuildSteps.description:
Describes how the canister should be built into WebAssembly,
including the adapters and build steps responsible for the build.

build.description:
The build configuration specifying how to compile the canister's source
code into a WebAssembly module, including the adapter to use.

sync.description: The configuration specifying how to sync the canister

CanisterManifest.description:
Represents the manifest describing a single canister.
This struct is typically loaded from a `canister.yaml` file and defines
the canister's name and how it should be built into WebAssembly.

init_args.description: Initialization arguments passed to the canister during installation.

name.description: The unique name of the canister as defined in this manifest.

settings.description: The configuration specifying the various settings when creating the canister.

api-url.description: The URL of the API endpoint. Should support the standard API routes (e.g. /api/v3).

http-gateway-url.description:
The URL of the HTTP gateway endpoint. Should support prefixing canister IDs as subdomains,
otherwise icp-cli will fall back to ?canisterId= query parameters which are frequently brittle in frontend code.

If no HTTP gateway endpoint is provided, canister URLs will not be printed in deploy operations.

url.description:
The URL this network can be reached at.

Assumed to be the URL of both the HTTP gateway (canister-id.domain.com) and API (domain.com/api/v3).

root-key.description: The root key of this network

CyclesAmount.description:
An amount of cycles.

Deserializes from a number or a string with suffixes (k, m, b, t) and optional underscore separators.

DurationAmount.description:
A duration in seconds.

Deserializes from a number (seconds) or a string with duration suffix (s, m, h, d, w)
and optional underscore separators.

Suffixes (case-insensitive):
- `s` — seconds
- `m` — minutes (×60)
- `h` — hours (×3600)
- `d` — days (×86400)
- `w` — weeks (×604800)

A bare number without suffix is treated as seconds.

canisters.description:
An optional list of the canisters to be included in this environments.
Defaults to all the canisters if not specified.

init_args.description: Override init args for specific canisters in this environment

network.description:
The target network for canister deployment.
Defaults to the `local` network if not specified

settings.description: Override the canister settings for this environment

bind.description: Network interface for the gateway. Defaults to 127.0.0.1

domains.description: Domains the gateway should respond to. Automatically includes localhost if applicable.

port.description: Port for the gateway to listen on. Defaults to 8000

path.description: Local path on-disk to read a WASM file from

LogVisibility.description: Controls who can read canister logs.

oneOf.description: Simple log visibility: 'controllers' (only controllers can view) or 'public' (anyone can view)
Specific principals that can view logs

allowed_viewers.description: List of principal IDs that can view canister logs

items.description: A principal ID that can view logs

args.description: Command line arguments to pass to the container's entrypoint

entrypoint.description: Entrypoint to use for the container

environment.description: Environment variables to set in the container in VAR=VALUE format (or VAR to inherit from host)

extra-hosts.description: Extra hosts entries for Docker networking (e.g. "host.docker.internal:host-gateway")

image.description: The docker image to use for the network

mounts.description: Bind mounts to add to the container in the format relative_host_path:container_path[:options]

platform.description: The platform to use for the container (e.g. linux/amd64)

rm-on-exit.description: Whether to delete the container when the network stops

status-dir.description: The status directory inside the container. Defaults to /app/status

user.description: The user to run the container as in the format user[:group]

volumes.description: Volumes to mount into the container in the format name:container_path[:options]

artificial-delay-ms.description: Artificial delay to add to every update call

bitcoind-addr.description: Bitcoin P2P node addresses to connect to (e.g. "127.0.0.1:18444")

dogecoind-addr.description: Dogecoin P2P node addresses to connect to

ii.description:
Set up the Internet Identity canister. Makes internet identity available at
id.ai.localhost:<port>

subnets.description: Configure the list of subnets (one application subnet by default)

version.description: The version of icp-cli-network-launcher to use. Defaults to the latest released version. Launcher versions correspond to published PocketIC or IC-OS releases.

anyOf.description: Plain string shorthand — treated as Candid.

ManifestInitArgs.description:
Init args as specified in a manifest file (canister.yaml or icp.yaml).

A plain string is shorthand for inline Candid:
```yaml
init_args: "(42)"
```

Object forms with explicit source and format:
```yaml
init_args:
  path: ./args.bin
  format: bin
```
```yaml
init_args:
  value: "(42)"
  format: candid
```

MemoryAmount.description:
An amount of memory in bytes.

Deserializes from a number or a string with suffixes (kb, kib, mb, mib, gb, gib),
optional decimals, and optional underscore separators.

NetworkManifest.description: A network definition for the project

sha256.description:
Optional sha256 checksum for the recipe template.
If provided, the integrity of the recipe will be verified against this hash

type.description:
An identifier for a recipe, it can have one of the following formats:

`file://<path_to_recipe>` - point to a local recipe template

`http://<url_to_recipe>` - point to a remote recipe template

`@<registry>/<recipe_name>@<version>` - Point to a recipe in a known registry.

For now the only registry is the `dfinity` registry at https://github.com/dfinity/icp-cli-recipes

It is recommended to also provide a hash in the `sha256` field

url.description: Url to fetch the remote WASM file from

Settings.description: Canister settings, such as compute and memory allocation.

compute_allocation.description: Compute allocation (0 to 100). Represents guaranteed compute capacity.

environment_variables.description:
Environment variables for the canister as key-value pairs.
These variables are accessible within the canister and can be used to configure
behavior without hardcoding values in the WASM module.

freezing_threshold.description:
Freezing threshold in seconds. Controls how long a canister can be inactive before being frozen.
Supports duration suffixes in YAML: s, m, h, d, w (e.g. "30d" or "4w").

log_memory_limit.description:
Log memory limit in bytes (max 2 MiB). Oldest logs are purged when usage exceeds this value.
Supports suffixes in YAML: kb, kib, mb, mib (e.g. "2mib" or "256kib"). Canister default is 4096 bytes.

log_visibility.description: Controls who can read canister logs.

memory_allocation.description:
Memory allocation in bytes. If unset, memory is allocated dynamically.
Supports suffixes in YAML: kb, kib, mb, mib, gb, gib (e.g. "4gib" or "2.5kb").

reserved_cycles_limit.description:
Upper limit on cycles reserved for future resource payments.
Memory allocations that would push the reserved balance above this limit will fail.
Supports suffixes in YAML: k, m, b, t (e.g. "4t" or "4.3t").

wasm_memory_limit.description:
Wasm memory limit in bytes. Sets an upper bound for Wasm heap growth.
Supports suffixes in YAML: kb, kib, mb, mib, gb, gib (e.g. "4gib" or "2.5kb").

wasm_memory_threshold.description:
Wasm memory threshold in bytes. Triggers a callback when exceeded.
Supports suffixes in YAML: kb, kib, mb, mib, gb, gib (e.g. "4gib" or "2.5kb").

SyncStep.description:
Identifies the type of adapter used to sync the canister,
along with its configuration.

The adapter type is specified via the `type` field in the YAML file.
For example:

```yaml
type: script
command: echo "synchronizing canister"
```

oneOf.description:
Represents a canister synced using a custom script or command.
This variant allows for flexible sync processes defined by the user.
Represents syncing of an assets canister

Represents a sync step executed by a WebAssembly plugin running inside
a wasmtime WASI sandbox.  The plugin can call canister methods on exactly
the canister being synced and read files from the declared `dirs`.

SyncSteps.description: Describes how to synchronize the canister state after deployment.
